seo.title: 100 Nuxt resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Nuxt resources for engineers.

content.intro: Nuxt 3 introduces the Nitro server engine and a complete shift to the Composition API, requiring a new approach to full-stack development. This guide focuses on technical implementation for SSR optimization, type-safe data fetching with Drizzle and Prisma, and deploying AI-integrated applications to edge runtimes like Vercel and Netlify.

items.title: Nuxt Bridge Implementation
items.description: Utilize @nuxt/bridge to access Nuxt 3 features in Nuxt 2 projects, including the Composition API and Nitro server engine, without a full rewrite.

items.title: Nitro Server Plugins
items.description: Create custom runtime logic by adding scripts to the /server/plugins directory to hook into Nitro's lifecycle events like 'render:html'.

items.title: Hybrid Rendering via routeRules
items.description: Configure the 'routeRules' key in nuxt.config.ts to define per-route caching strategies, including ISR, SWR, and SPA-only rendering.

items.title: Typed Runtime Config
items.description: Define 'runtimeConfig' in your configuration and use 'useRuntimeConfig()' to access environment variables safely on both server and client.

items.title: Nuxt Layers for Multi-App Architecture
items.description: Extract shared components, composables, and server logic into a base layer that can be extended by multiple Nuxt applications using the 'extends' property.

items.title: App Config vs Runtime Config
items.description: Use 'app.config.ts' for public, reactive UI configuration and 'runtimeConfig' for sensitive, environment-specific variables like API keys.

items.title: Middleware Route Guards
items.description: Implement 'defineNuxtRouteMiddleware' in the /middleware directory to handle authentication and redirection logic before a page renders.

items.title: Custom Nuxt Modules
items.description: Develop local modules in the /modules directory to automate repetitive tasks like injecting global CSS or registering third-party plugins.

items.title: Nuxt DevTools Integration
items.description: Enable Nuxt DevTools in development to inspect component hierarchies, check state hydration, and analyze bundle sizes in real-time.

items.title: Server-Side Error Boundaries
items.description: Use 'clearError' and 'showError' composables to manage application-level errors and prevent full-page crashes during SSR failures.

items.title: Drizzle ORM with Nitro
items.description: Integrate Drizzle ORM within /server/api routes for type-safe SQL queries against PostgreSQL or MySQL databases using drivers like 'postgres' or 'mysql2'.

items.title: Pinia Server-Side Hydration
items.description: Synchronize Pinia state between server and client by utilizing the Nuxt-specific Pinia plugin to prevent hydration mismatch errors.

items.title: Prisma Client Management
items.description: Instantiate the Prisma client in a server utility file (/server/utils) to ensure a single connection pool across Nitro server routes.

items.title: Supabase Auth with Nuxt
items.description: Leverage the @nuxtjs/supabase module to manage user sessions and protect server routes using the 'serverSupabaseUser' helper.

items.title: Zod Validation in Nitro
items.description: Use Zod to schema-validate 'readBody(event)' data in server routes to ensure incoming POST requests meet strict type requirements.

items.title: Nuxt Content for CMS
items.description: Implement @nuxt/content to manage Markdown, YAML, or CSV files as a Git-based CMS with built-in search and query capabilities.

items.title: Unstorage for Server Caching
items.description: Utilize the built-in 'unstorage' layer in Nitro to implement multi-driver caching (Redis, FS, memory) for expensive API calls.

items.title: SWR Data Fetching
items.description: Use 'useFetch' with the 'key' parameter to implement stale-while-revalidate patterns, reducing perceived latency for dynamic data.

items.title: Stripe Webhook Handling
items.description: Create a raw server route in /server/api/webhooks to process Stripe events using 'readRawBody' for signature verification.

items.title: Optimistic UI with Pinia
items.description: Implement manual state updates in Pinia stores before server confirmation to create instantaneous feedback for user actions.

items.title: Vercel AI SDK Integration
items.description: Implement streaming responses from OpenAI or Anthropic in Nuxt server routes using the 'StreamingTextResponse' utility for real-time AI chat.

items.title: NuxtUI Component Library
items.description: Utilize @nuxt/ui for accessible, Tailwind-based components that are specifically optimized for Nuxt 3's auto-import system.

items.title: Nuxt Image Provider Setup
items.description: Configure @nuxt/image with providers like Cloudinary or Vercel to automatically resize and serve WebP/AVIF formats based on client headers.

items.title: Tailwind Typography for AI Content
items.description: Apply the '@tailwindcss/typography' plugin to style raw Markdown output generated by LLMs within Nuxt pages.

items.title: Server-Side PDF Generation
items.description: Use 'puppeteer-core' within a Nitro server route to generate dynamic PDFs from Nuxt components for invoices or reports.

items.title: Nuxt SEO Module
items.description: Automate sitemap generation, robots.txt, and JSON-LD schema using the 'nuxt-seo' module collection to improve search rankings.

items.title: Edge Function Deployment
items.description: Optimize Nuxt for Vercel Edge Functions by ensuring server routes do not use Node.js-specific APIs like 'fs' or 'path'.

items.title: VueUse for Nuxt
items.description: Integrate @vueuse/nuxt to access 200+ composition utilities like 'useIntersectionObserver' or 'useDark' without manual configuration.

items.title: Nuxt Icon with Iconify
items.description: Use the 'nuxt-icon' module to load any icon from the Iconify set on-demand, preventing large SVG bundles in the client build.

items.title: Bundle Analysis with Nuxi
items.description: Run 'nuxi analyze' to visualize the client and server bundles, identifying large dependencies like 'moment' or 'lodash' for removal.

pro_tips: Always use 'useFetch' or 'useAsyncData' for data fetching in components to ensure data is serialized and hydrated correctly without double-invoking on the client.
Place shared server logic in the 'server/utils' directory; Nitro automatically imports these files, making them available across all API routes.
Use the 'shallowRef' instead of 'ref' for large data objects (like AI responses) to avoid unnecessary deep reactivity overhead.
Leverage 'defineRouteRules' to set 'cors: true' for specific API endpoints to allow cross-origin requests without a global middleware.
When using Tailwind CSS, enable the 'viewer' option in 'nuxt.config' to access a local dashboard of your design tokens at /_tailwind/.
